name: Identity Resolution Hub - Backstory Starter
description: Resolves people, accounts, owners, and channel identities across CRM, messaging, and meeting systems into a canonical identity layer.

nodes.name: Identity Candidate Webhook
nodes.notes: Receive person, account, owner, and channel identity candidates from CRM, messaging, or meeting systems.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Keep merge thresholds and review destinations outside the workflow so identity policy can evolve without reauthoring the core logic.

nodes.name: Normalize Identity Signals
nodes.notes: Flatten identity records into a canonical candidate shape before applying merge policy.

nodes.name: Review Ambiguities

parameters.text:
=Assess this identity candidate for canonical matching.

Candidate: {{ JSON.stringify($json, null, 2) }}

Return:
- likely canonical person or account match
- whether the match is safe to auto-merge
- ambiguity risks and manual-review reasons
- downstream workflow impact if matched incorrectly

options.systemMessage: You are an identity resolution assistant that preserves canonical person, account, owner, and channel relationships across systems.

nodes.notes: Use this step to explain alias collisions, duplicate humans, and shared-mailbox risks before writing the identity graph.

nodes.name: Publish Identity Update
nodes.notes: Replace this with your identity graph, database, or review-queue destination. Split auto-merge vs manual-review behavior after the policy is finalized.
